Treatment of Taiwan Relating to Transfers of Defense Articles and Defense Services, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–228, div. B, title XII, § 1206116 Stat. 1428Sept. 30, 2002“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for purposes of the transfer or possible transfer of defense articles or defense services under the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.), the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.), or any other provision of law, Taiwan shall be treated as though it were designated a major non-NATO ally (as defined in section 644(q) of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ()[)].”22 U.S.C. 275122 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2403(q)[For definitions of “defense article” and “defense service” as used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under .]section 1206 of Pub. Presidential Determinations Relating to Designations of Countries as Major Non-NATO AlliesThe following Presidential Determinations related to the designation or termination of the countries listed as major non-NATO allies of the United States for purposes of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) and the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.):22 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2751.—Determination No. 2012–11, dated , released , not published in Federal Register. Terminated by Determination No. 2022–24, , 87 F.R. 60057.AfghanistanJuly 6, 2012July 7, 2012Sept. 23, 2022.—Determination No. 98–9, , 63 F.R. 3635.ArgentinaJan. 6, 1998.—Determination No. 2002–10, , 67 F.R. 13247.BahrainMar. 14, 2002.—Determination No. 2019–21, , 84 F.R. 43035.BrazilJuly 31, 2019.—Determination No. 2022–14, , 87 F.R. 32943.ColombiaMay 23, 2022.—Determination No. 97–4, , 61 F.R. 59809.JordanNov. 12, 1996.—Determination No. 2024–05, , 89 F.R. 57055.KenyaJune 24, 2024.—Determination No. 2004–21, , 69 F.R. 4843.KuwaitJan. 15, 2004.—Determination No. 2004–35, , 69 F.R. 34049.MoroccoJune 3, 2004.—Determination No. 2004–37, , 69 F.R. 38797.PakistanJune 16, 2004.—Determination No. 2004–02, , 68 F.R. 59855.PhilippinesOct. 6, 2003.—Determination No. 2022–10, , 87 F.R. 15025.QatarMar. 10, 2022.—Determination No. 2004–16, , 69 F.R. 2053.ThailandDec. 30, 2003.—Determination No. 2015–09, , 80 F.R. 43613.TunisiaJuly 10, 2015

children.text: designating a country as a major non-NATO ally for purposes of this chapter and the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.); or22 U.S.C. 2751

children.heading: Initial designations
children.label: b Initial designations
children.text: Australia, Egypt, Israel, Japan, the Republic of Korea, and New Zealand shall be deemed to have been so designated by the President as of the effective date of this section, and the President is not required to notify the Congress of such designation of those countries.
